title: Peabo Bryson, singer behind beloved Disney classics, dies at 75
description: Bryson was best known as the voice behind Disney songs like "A Whole New World" and "Beauty and the Beast".
content:
R&B singer Peabo Bryson, best known for his voice on Disney classics like A Whole New World and Beauty and the Beast, has died aged 75.
He died Tuesday evening surrounded by family and loved ones, his family said in a statement.
A cause of death has ... [320 chars]

title: US and Iran launch fresh strikes amid stalled ceasefire talks
description: US military says it struck tanker and sites on Iran’s Qeshm Island and defended Kuwait and Bahrain from missile attacks
content:
The US and Iran have exchanged fresh missiles and drone strikes, further jeopardising efforts by Washington to secure a new ceasefire agreement with Tehran.
US forces fired a Hellfire missile to disable a tanker attempting to break through the Americ... [3917 chars]

title: Putin remains uncompromising on Ukraine, but is public discourse on war changing in Russia?
description: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.
content:
If Vladimir Putin's Russia had an official slogan, what would it be?
"Russia is what it is, and we're not ashamed of showing it," Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov once told me in an interview.
That fits.
But I recently heard an updated version from vet... [6168 chars]

title: Three quarters of workers not on track for 'moderate' pension income, report suggests
description: A newly-published report suggests a moderate lifestyle in retirement costs £32,700 for one person and £45,400 for two.
content:
Too many people face a "cliff-edge drop in income" when they retire, with more than three quarters not on course to save enough for a "moderate" lifestyle, a pensions trade body has warned.
A new report by Pensions UK suggested what it termed a moder... [1451 chars]

title: Puffin and bumblebee among 18 creatures shortlisted to feature on banknotes
description: The Bank of England is asking the public which animals should appear on future banknotes.
content:
Eighteen animals, birds and insects have been shortlisted to appear on future banknotes - and the public can have their say on which creatures feature.
The wildlife beauty contest gives the colourful kingfisher and common frog an equal chance of a pl... [2708 chars]
